description: Trust gating: an undeclared (untrusted) authserv-id reports DMARC pass with header.from=evil.test while the visible From is example.com. Because header.from is not cryptographic, a forge-able untrusted header's value is just the attacker's own assertion, so it is excluded from dmarcHeaderFromDomains entirely — the list is empty and dmarcHeaderFromMatchesFromDomain is null, emitting no dmarc.headerFromMismatch signal. Only untrustedAuthservIdRule flags the forge-able source. Confirms an untrusted DMARC context cannot manufacture a noisy consistency signal.

authentication-results: relay.evil.test; dmarc=pass header.from=evil.test; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com

authenticationResults.raw: relay.evil.test; dmarc=pass header.from=evil.test; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com

signals.message: Authentication-Results header was produced by an untrusted authserv-id.
